### Post by buried on 2008-03-27

I have enabled restricted ATi drivers.
Installed Fglrx, compiz stuff etc...
Update and Restart.
error I got:
Desktop effects could not be enabled.
here is my Xorg.conf
```
# xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"	"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"	"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"	"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizEdgeScroll"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies Inc RS485 [Radeon Xpress 1100 IGP]"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	Busid		"PCI:1:5:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"ATI Technologies Inc RS485 [Radeon Xpress 1100 IGP]"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	Defaultdepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
  screen "Default Screen"
	Inputdevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	Inputdevice	"Configured Mouse"
	
	# Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
	#	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
	Inputdevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection
Section "Module"
	Load		"glx"
EndSection
Section "Extensions"
	Option		"Composite"	"0"
EndSection
```

mitchell@mitchell-laptop:~$ /usr/bin/compiz

### Post by Ub1476 on 2008-03-27
XGL is the only way it works if you use the driver from the repos. You have to logout and login for it to take effect. If you want to use the latest driver from ATI (which works without XGL), download Envy and let it fetch the driver and configure X for you. You have to remove the driver from Restricted Driver Manager first though (reboot is not necessary before Envy has installed the driver).

If you have Radeon rv350 or a rv450 card, it's blacklisted. There's a sticky describing it in this forum.

---

### Post by buried on 2008-03-27

If my last choice is to install the ATI driver, then well.

### Post by mrmiserable on 2008-03-27
you need to whitelist fglrx driver 

sudo gedit /usr/bin/compiz

add fglrx to line 54 i think 

 # Driver whitelist
WHITELIST="nvidia intel ati radeon i810 fglrx"

then restart compiz

hope it helps

---

### Post by AdrianStrays on 2008-03-28

---

### Post by buried on 2008-03-29
Solved, re-installed Ubuntu and turned on Internet Connection during installation.
it was my last choice.
